etymology.description: Original: 爪(爪, "hands") + 角(角, "horn") + 女(女, "woman") -> Later form: 母(母, "mother") + 中(中) + 女(女, "daughter")
etymology.interpretation: The character 婁(lóu) likely originated as a phono-semantic compound, combining 爪(爪, "hands") to depict manipulation or bundling, with the phonetic support of 角(角), and 女(女, "woman") as another semantic component. Over time, its representation evolved into an ideogrammic compound with 母(母, "mother"), 中(中) suggesting centrality or encompassing, and 女(女, "daughter"), potentially symbolizing the concept of bringing things together or bundling, which fits its meaning as 'bundle'.
